title: Database failover during peak traffic
summary: Proactive analysis of potential database failover scenarios during Black Friday traffic peaks. Our primary database has never been tested under 10x normal load with a simultaneous failover event.
customer_impact_summary: If failover fails or takes too long, customers would see errors during checkout, potentially losing orders during peak revenue period.
internal_impact_summary: Engineering team would need all-hands response. Potential revenue loss in the millions.
contributing_factors: Failover has never been tested under peak load conditions
Read replicas may not handle full write load if promoted
Connection pool settings optimized for normal traffic, not failover surge
Monitoring alerts tuned for gradual degradation, not sudden failover
prevention_gaps: No automated failover testing in production-like load
Runbook assumes operator availability within 5 minutes
No circuit breaker between application and database layer

hypotheses.description: Connection pool exhaustion during failover will cause cascading failures
Read replica promotion will take longer than application timeout settings
Monitoring will not detect partial failures fast enough

timeline.description: Premortem session initiated for Black Friday readiness
Historical analysis: last failover took 45 seconds under normal load
Load test results reviewed: 10x traffic causes 3x connection pool usage

action_items.description: Conduct failover drill under simulated peak load in staging
Increase connection pool size and add circuit breaker
Reduce application database timeout from 30s to 10s
Add failover-specific alerting with 10-second detection threshold

evidence.description: Connection pool utilization during last year's Black Friday
